extraction.one_line_summary: DILLIER won from 190km+ breakaway, outsprinting STUYVEN who had teammate PEDERSEN doing work but couldn't convert superior speed.
tactical_patterns: PEDERSEN sacrificed own chance doing late pace work for STUYVEN, who still lost sprint—domestique deployment timing error.
YATES Adam bridged to breakaway late despite being GC contender, suggesting Sky willing to burn matches offensively early.
QuickStep controlled peloton despite only defending pink, Sky joined pace—both superteams marked each other into neutrality.

rider_signals.DILLIER Silvan: positive: converted 200km+ breakaway into win despite inferior sprint credentials, strong late-stage reserves
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: negative: failed to win with team support and superior sprint speed after 190km, poor energy management
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: neutral: worked for STUYVEN instead of going for own result, reveals Trek hierarchy clear
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: attacked to join breakaway as GC rider, signals Sky using offensive tactics not just Froome defense
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: neutral: retained pink jersey by 6 seconds only, vulnerable margin heading into mountains
